identity.name: Mother Rampion
identity.kind: witch, retired — keeps the walled garden now
identity.story: Grew the finest rampion in three tales and priced it harder than any greens are worth — the bargain that put a girl up a tower for want of a salad. Keeps a walled plot behind the cottage now, gate left open for anyone who only wants to ask this time. Never harvests the one row nearest the wall; lets it go to seed every year and has never said why, though the whole lane has a guess.

relationships.kind: grew the rampion that started the whole bargain, and leaves the tower windowbox be every high sun without a word — the one thing between them neither has finished saying; this lamplighting the cutting sat in her hand a beat too long, the Princess reaching past it instead, straight for the one row that's never once been harvested — and what she took never made it home, only got set down on the wall between them, in the open, the first thing off that row anyone's touched on purpose since it was let go to seed. Mother Rampion didn't reach to stop it landing there, and hasn't moved it since
matches her turnip count row for row at the garden fence, and privately thinks her own rampion's better, which she'll never say

behavior.label: Mother Rampion's plot, kept open at the gate

child.label: walking the wall's edge before the dew's off it

children.label: training the one row of rampion she still lets go to seed
carrying a fresh cutting up to the lane
holding the fresh cutting out a beat too long, watching the Princess's hand go past it for the seeded row instead — still no word for what any of it's meant to say
finding a single seed-head set on the garden wall afterward, off the one row she's never let a basket near — not reaching to move it, not reaching to keep it either

child.label: banking the coals under a plot that keeps no secrets anymore
